title: Logo + Four Challenge-Coin Marks: Concept Rasters to Coin-Ready Vector Artwork with Black/White/Gold/Silver Variants
grounding_note: Verbatim from the real Upwork brief (cand_38, db_i 95): client already HAS concept images and visual references; needs them turned into "polished, professional, true vector artwork" for a logo plus "four symbolic challenge coin designs"; must "work in black, white, gold, and silver", be "readable at small sizes", be "coin-ready artwork that can be sent to a mint/manufacturer"; deliverables explicitly list SVG, transparent PNG exports, and black/white/gold/silver versions; fixed price $400, "first production-ready vector artwork phase". Reasonably enriched for a runnable connector benchmark: I specify the five concrete source rasters (1 logo + 4 coin references) the client supplies, fix the export canvas at 2000x2000 px square for coin proofs, and name the exact color values (true black #000000, white #FFFFFF, gold #C8A24B, silver/gray #AAAAAA) used for the mono variants. The brief's "editable Illustrator / EPS / vector PDF" deliverables are honored by producing genuine SVG vector output via image_vectorize (the connector's true raster-to-vector path) and bundling the family in one packaging step; I do NOT claim from-scratch illustration — every vector is traced from a client-provided reference, which is exactly what the brief asks ("turn those concepts into... true vector artwork").
one_line_ask: Turn the client's five supplied concept rasters (one logo + four challenge-coin references) into clean, coin-ready vector marks, each delivered as transparent-PNG and SVG in black, white, gold, and silver.
full_brief:
A challenge-coin / branding client (automotive-industrial sector) supplies five flat reference images: one primary logo concept and four symbolic coin-face concepts. They are pixelated mock-ups that cannot go to a mint. The task is to convert each into production-grade vector art and deliver a four-color family per mark.

Pipeline per the connector chain: (1) upload all five client rasters to CC storage and preview them; (2) on the primary logo, remove the busy background so only the mark survives on transparency; (3) auto-straighten the de-bagged logo so the mark sits square (coins are minted on a true axis); (4) crop the straightened logo tight to its own bounds so the trace has no dead margin; (5) reframe/resize that cropped mark to a fixed 2000x2000 px square proof canvas via image_crop_and_resize (a gen-free crop-and-scale to the target aspect — no pixels are invented), establishing the exact resolution every variant export inherits; (6) vectorize that clean, square, transparent master into a scalable SVG — this is the editable master vector that also seeds EPS/PDF on packaging; (7) select the mark's silhouette on the 2000x2000 master, then fill it with solid true-black to mint the BLACK variant; (8) on the same selected silhouette, fill it with solid white to mint the WHITE variant; (9) apply a flat color overlay in gold #C8A24B to mint the GOLD variant; (10) apply a monochromatic silver/gray tint to mint the SILVER variant. Then the four coin references are run through the same remove-background -> crop -> vectorize spine so all five marks are coin-ready vector. Finally, copy every produced vector + variant asset into a structured per-mark deliverables folder and package the full logo+four-coin family (SVG sources, transparent PNGs, black/white/gold/silver variants) into one ZIP for mint/manufacturer handoff.

Hard constraints honored: no from-scratch illustration (every mark is traced from a client reference), no generative fill / outpaint / object removal / compositing / upscaling; image_crop_and_resize only crops-and-scales the existing mark to the square proof canvas (it never grows or invents canvas), image_fill_area is used only for SOLID color minting (black and white) of an isolated mask, and no generative tool is used at any step. Deliverables map 1:1 to the brief: transparent PNGs, SVG vector source, and black/white/gold/silver color versions, coin-ready and readable at small sizes.

inputs.name: logo_concept_primary.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat, slightly pixelated client mock-up of a premium automotive-industrial brand logo: a bold heraldic shield containing a stylized piston-and-gear motif with the wordmark 'IRONLINE FORGE' in a serious condensed sans-serif beneath it. Rendered as a low-resolution raster concept on a cluttered dark workshop photo background (not transparent), uneven lighting, slight JPEG-style artifacts, the kind of reference a client emails before hiring a vector designer. Crisp enough that the silhouette is legible but clearly raster, not vector.
inputs.realism_notes: Must read as a real client-supplied raster mock-up, NOT a finished vector: include a non-transparent busy background (so remove_background has real work), mild compression artifacts, and a clear symbolic silhouette. Text-bearing wordmark -> gemini-3-pro-image.

inputs.name: coin_ref_01_eagle.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Flat raster concept for a challenge-coin face: a symbolic spread-winged eagle gripping a wrench and a lightning bolt, circular emblem composition, drawn as a single-color symbolic mark on a plain but slightly noisy off-white scanned-paper background. Low-res reference quality, visible pixel edges, the kind of hand-mocked symbol a client provides for vectorization.
inputs.realism_notes: Strong symbolic silhouette suitable for coin minting; non-transparent paper background so background removal is genuine; small embedded micro-text on a banner to stress 'readable at small sizes'.

inputs.name: coin_ref_02_gear_globe.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Flat raster concept for a challenge-coin face: a globe wrapped by a mechanical gear ring with two crossed industrial hammers behind it, circular symbolic emblem, single-tone, on a flat mid-gray background that is NOT transparent. Deliberately low-resolution, soft pixelated edges, client-reference look.
inputs.realism_notes: Concentric circular composition tests crop-to-bounds and vectorize on fine internal detail (gear teeth).

inputs.name: coin_ref_03_anvil.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Flat raster concept for a challenge-coin face: a heavy anvil with a forging hammer crossed over it and a rope-border ring, bold symbolic emblem, single dark tone on a textured beige background (non-transparent). Low-res, slightly blurry, a reference mock-up to be turned into vector.
inputs.realism_notes: Rope-border ring is a classic coin edge motif and stresses the vectorizer on repeating fine geometry.

inputs.name: coin_ref_04_tractor.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Flat raster concept for a challenge-coin face: a side-profile agricultural tractor silhouette inside a wheat-wreath circular border with a small star at the top, symbolic single-color emblem on a plain but faintly gradient blue background (non-transparent). Low-resolution client reference, visible aliasing.
inputs.realism_notes: Ties the agriculture vertical to the automotive/industrial set; wheat-wreath border is fine detail for the trace; gradient background makes background removal non-trivial.

outputs.name: logo_vector_master.svg
outputs.spec: Clean scalable SVG traced from the cropped, background-free, straightened, 2000x2000-normalized primary logo; editable master vector (seeds EPS / vector-PDF on packaging); coin-ready, readable at <=15 mm.

outputs.name: logo_variant_black.png
outputs.spec: Transparent PNG, 2000x2000 px, logo silhouette filled solid true-black #000000.

outputs.name: logo_variant_white.png
outputs.spec: Transparent PNG, 2000x2000 px, logo silhouette filled solid white #FFFFFF (the on-light / reversed variant).

outputs.name: logo_variant_gold.png
outputs.spec: Transparent PNG, 2000x2000 px, logo flat gold #C8A24B color overlay.

outputs.name: logo_variant_silver.png
outputs.spec: Transparent PNG, 2000x2000 px, logo silver/gray #AAAAAA monochromatic tint.

outputs.name: coin_vectors_x4.svg
outputs.spec: Four clean SVG coin-face vectors (eagle, gear-globe, anvil, tractor), each traced from its background-removed reference, coin-ready for mint review.

outputs.name: ironline_coin_package.zip
outputs.spec: Single ZIP bundling all SVG vector sources, transparent PNG exports, and the black/white/gold/silver variant set, organized per-mark for mint/manufacturer handoff.

connector_workflow.note: Initialize a CC-storage upload session for the five client-supplied concept rasters (logo + 4 coin refs).
Finalize the upload; returns stored asset handles/URLs for all five rasters.
Preview the primary logo raster to confirm the mark and its busy background before processing (inspect only).
Strip the cluttered workshop background from the primary logo -> transparent PNG of the bare mark.
Auto-straighten the isolated mark so it sits on a true axis (coins mint on-axis).
Crop tight to the mark's own bounds, removing dead transparent margin so the trace is clean.

Reframe/scale the cropped mark to a fixed 2000x2000 px square proof canvas (gen-free crop-and-scale to target aspect; no pixels invented). Establishes the exact resolution every variant export inherits.
Trace the clean, square, transparent logo master into a scalable SVG -> the editable master vector deliverable.
Select the logo silhouette on the 2000x2000 master to drive the solid-color minting of the black and white variants.
Solid-fill the selected silhouette with true-black #000000 -> BLACK variant (image_fill_area = solid color only), 2000x2000 px.
Solid-fill the same selected silhouette with white #FFFFFF -> WHITE / reversed variant (image_fill_area = solid color only), 2000x2000 px.
Apply a flat gold #C8A24B color overlay to the master -> GOLD variant, 2000x2000 px.
Apply a silver/gray #AAAAAA monochromatic tint -> SILVER variant, 2000x2000 px.
Run the four coin references through background removal (eagle / gear-globe / anvil / tractor), one call per image -> transparent coin faces.
Crop each coin face tight to bounds before tracing (one call per image).
Trace the four cropped coin faces into clean coin-ready SVG vectors (one vectorize call per coin; four outputs collected under this handle).
Copy all produced vectors + variant PNGs into a structured per-mark deliverables folder in CC storage.
Single unavoidable local packaging step: zip the organized SVG/PNG/variant family into one mint-handoff archive.

chaining_note: See connector_workflow inputs_from edges. The 2000x2000 proof canvas is fixed once at step 7 (image_crop_and_resize) and inherited by the SVG trace and all four color variants; black and white share one image_select_subject mask (steps 9-11).
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon (18 steps, 13 distinct Adobe tools) spanning asset I/O, raster cleanup, geometry, raster-to-vector, masked solid minting, and per-mark packaging. Difficulty comes from the two parallel chained spines (logo + four coins) plus a three-way variant fan-out from a single cropped master, all of which must stay on-axis and crop-tight so the trace yields mint-grade vectors readable at small sizes. It is faithful to the brief (refine provided concepts into true vector art; black/white/gold/silver; SVG + transparent PNG; coin-ready) while respecting every capability limit: vectorize is the genuine raster->vector path, fill_area mints only solid color on an isolated mask, color_overlay/monochromatic_tint do the metallic tints, and no banned capability (gen-fill, object removal, compositing, text-to-image, upscaling, layout composition) appears. Only one unavoidable [L] step (final zip); all 17 core steps are headless-confirmed [C].

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capability present. image_crop_and_resize (step 7) only crops-and-scales the existing cropped mark to a 2000x2000 square proof canvas — it never grows/outpaints/invents canvas or upscales beyond a reframe of the source. image_fill_area (steps 10-11) does solid black and solid white fills of an isolated image_select_subject mask only. image_vectorize is the genuine raster-to-vector path; every mark is traced from a client-supplied reference (no from-scratch art/logo/text-to-image). No image_crop_and_resize/reframe-crop, no object-removal-with-reconstruction, no super-resolution/upscale, no compositing. image_apply_lens_blur is not used. asset_preview_file is inspect-only and emits no new file. All 17 core steps are real Adobe-connector actions (13 distinct Adobe tools) plus one unavoidable local zip.
reverify.notes: Fixed both flagged completeness nits: (1) added image_crop_and_resize (step 7) to establish the asserted 2000x2000 px canvas the variants inherit, resolving the undefined-dimension gap; (2) added a produced WHITE #FFFFFF variant via a second solid image_fill_area (step 11) plus its logo_variant_white.png output, so all four brief-listed color versions (black/white/gold/silver) are actually generated rather than documented-only. Reconciled full_brief, outputs, and chaining_note to describe the crop-and-resize reframe (not any generative sizing) and the real four-fill/tint family. Brand IRONLINE FORGE is an invented, non-colliding fictional name (no real company match); no real people depicted; kept as-is.
